mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which typically raises the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS has risen over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, which leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.4 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the UNRATE signal.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth combined with accelerating consumer price inflation indicates an overheating economy, which forces the central bank to implement more aggressive monetary tightening, thereby negatively impacting equity valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 6% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of periods where both RSAFS and CPIAUCSL growth conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high levels of housing starts during an economic expansion reflect strong consumer and business confidence, indicating continued demand and investment, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1800 (thousands of units) for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during identified expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
regime_hint: any regime with positive job growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals a significant slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic activity, indicating weakening corporate earnings and potential recessionary pressures, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 0.7% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the INDPRO signal.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of persistently high inflation and a very low unemployment rate creates a 'hot' economy scenario, compelling the Federal Reserve to maintain or intensify restrictive monetary policy, which typically weighs heavily on equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: overheating economy with hawkish central bank
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of periods where both high inflation and low unemployment conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth in an environment of moderate inflation indicates a healthy and sustainable economic expansion, fostering strong corporate earnings and investor confidence, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth > 3.0% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 3.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during periods of stable, low-inflation expansion.
